selection.decision_rule: each contract independently tournaments every eligible complete MONOTONIC, MONOTONIC_RAW, raw-syscall, and architectural-counter path; a challenger wins only by > max(1 ns/read, 5%) in >=8/9 paired batches
selection.feat_sb: ineligible: Arm SB constrains side-channel-observable speculation but does not order architectural counter sampling after a prior Acquire observation
selection.kernel_errata: trapped CNTVCT/CNTVCTSS reads remain eligible because arm64 emulates them with its workaround-aware counter reader; exact-path measurement determines profitability

ordered_probe.sb_eligibility: ineligible: SB does not order architectural counter sampling

selection.permission_rule: PR_GET_TSC is authoritative when implemented, including Android/vendor backports; only exact -EINVAL plus a parsed upstream-pre-6.12 arm64 uname release infers legacy-safe counter access; newer, unknown, and other failed queries remain syscall-only
selection.post_init_boundary: PR_SET_TSC(PR_TSC_SIGSEGV) must not revoke counter access after direct-provider selection

selection.decision_rule: median advantage > max(1 ns/read, 5%) and >=8/9 decisive paired wins

wall_selection.decision_rule: each contract independently tournaments every eligible complete MONOTONIC, MONOTONIC_RAW, raw-syscall, and architectural-counter path; a challenger wins only by > max(1 ns/read, 5%) in >=8/9 paired batches
wall_selection.feat_sb: ineligible: Arm SB constrains side-channel-observable speculation but does not order architectural counter sampling after a prior Acquire observation
wall_selection.kernel_errata: trapped CNTVCT/CNTVCTSS reads remain eligible because arm64 emulates them with its workaround-aware counter reader; exact-path measurement determines profitability

ordered_probe.sb_eligibility: ineligible: SB does not order architectural counter sampling

wall_selection.permission_rule: PR_GET_TSC is authoritative when implemented, including Android/vendor backports; only exact -EINVAL plus a parsed upstream-pre-6.12 arm64 uname release infers legacy-safe counter access; newer, unknown, and other failed queries remain syscall-only
wall_selection.post_init_boundary: PR_SET_TSC(PR_TSC_SIGSEGV) must not revoke counter access after direct-provider selection

failure_fallback.eligibility_gate: perf task-clock mmap exposes complete seqlock conversion metadata and a usable architectural counter
failure_fallback.trigger: perf event or mmap capability is unavailable, or an inline mmap read fails

native_entry_probe.selection_basis: the inlined raw Linux syscall is the native primitive; libc wraps the same kernel clock and remains the failure fallback

perf.decision_rule: prefer perf task-clock mmap when its complete inline capability is available; otherwise use the selected native CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID entry; paired path samples audit but do not choose this policy
perf.measurement_clock: raw SYS_clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW), never libc/vDSO or the candidate under test

path_probe.selection_basis: complete perf mmap metadata and architectural-counter capability; audit samples do not select the provider

selection.read_cost_basis: perf mmap is Inline because tach issues no OS or host call on the hot path; persistent perf read and every CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID entry route are SystemCall
